title: Fiery Smoky American Padron Peppers Skillet
description: A pungent and crumbly one-pot American dish featuring padron peppers, robata-grill flavors, and a keto-friendly twist.
instructions: Preheat the robata grill to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the padron peppers with smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
Heat the bacon fat in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the coated padron peppers to the skillet and sear for 2 minutes on each side.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated robata grill and cook for 10 minutes, or until the peppers are slightly charred.
Remove the skillet from the grill and sprinkle the shredded cheddar cheese over the top of the peppers.
Return the skillet to the grill and cook for an additional 2 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the skillet from the grill and sprinkle the chopped cilantro and diced jalapeno peppers over the top.
Serve the skillet hot, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
